SEARCH
Package simulator.core

Class Scheduler

java.lang.Object
simulator.core.Scheduler
All Implemented Interfaces:
SchedulingStrategy
public class Scheduler extends Object implements SchedulingStrategy
Implements the Round-Robin scheduling algorithm with processor affinity. Manages a manual queue of processes in the READY state.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Scheduler()
     

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addProcess(Process p)
    Adds a process to the waiting queue (READY).
    void
    addProcessToFront(Process p)
    Adds a process directly to the front of the queue, giving it maximum priority.
    void
    schedule(Processor[] processors, SystemProcess sysProcess, MemoryManager memManager, int timeSlice, int globalTime, SimulationEngine engine)
    Executes the scheduling logic.

  • Constructor Details

    • Scheduler

      public Scheduler()

  • Method Details

    • addProcess

      public void addProcess(Process p)
      Adds a process to the waiting queue (READY).
      Parameters:
      p - The process to be added.

    • schedule

      public void schedule(Processor[] processors, SystemProcess sysProcess, MemoryManager memManager, int timeSlice, int globalTime, SimulationEngine engine)
      Executes the scheduling logic. Assigns processes to free processors. The system process has absolute priority if it is in the READY state.
      Specified by:
      schedule in interface SchedulingStrategy
      Parameters:
      processors - Array of physical processors.
      sysProcess - The system process (VIP).
      memManager - Memory manager (used to check if the process is in RAM).
      timeSlice - Allowed execution time quantum.

    • addProcessToFront

      public void addProcessToFront(Process p)
      Adds a process directly to the front of the queue, giving it maximum priority. Useful for processes that were just loaded into RAM (to avoid thrashing).
